title: Bike Touring - Bosnia/Herzegovina -  Serbia to Sarajevo. 5-2010

transcript.text: So I just crossed into Bosnia from Serbia.
This is that same river that I spent last night on.
I think it's called the River Dvorna, the name of Robert De Niro's daughter.
So we've got some buildings right on the river side.
It's pretty.
I'm right on this road down the side of it.
We had another road stop, a lot of these tunnels here along the mountainous river side.
You can see all the cars behind me.
Pack this up for us.
Typical Bosnian mountain village.
Lots of hills in Bosnia.
I've been climbing for a while now.
These barking dogs are pretty typical of Serbian Bosnia too.
And chickens too.
They're growing their own food.
I've never done that in America.
I would, but is that a cemetery?
Anyways, I'm still climbing.
And this is a cool little neighborhood, huh?
I like it.
I haven't painted the houses yet.
It's pretty.
Yeah, so I'm still climbing.
It's come 39 miles so far.
And I got a road on the road.
That's what's in it.
So out of here.
Here I am in the climb in Bosnia.
I'm at 3,150 feet.
It's higher than I've been since Austria.
The water bottle up and the water there.
And I'm about to get going again.
Serk Bosnia.
Check out the old time hay bales.
I haven't seen those.
Except in the movies.
Bosnia.
Coming down off the pass.
I'm in a different climactic zone now.
4,080 feet.
I was surrounded by jungle plants earlier today.
And now all of a sudden I notice that it's pine trees.
And there's the valley.
I don't know how many miles I'm outside of Sarajevo.
I'll probably get there at around dusk.
About to go down a steep descent.
It's pretty here, man.
Hills all day.
Bosnia.
17 miles outside of Sarajevo.
Look at the steamness of this ravine.
I love riding on terrain like this.
I can't tell you how fucking fun it is.
Just going uphill.
Look, it's a ski resort.
That's probably Olympic Hill.
You see?
Look, yeah.
God, I love roads like this.
I'm going to have a big downhill.
Okay.
So here's coming off of that pass.
I got to like 4,080 feet.
Which is almost as high as I did the day before I came in.
And now I'm at 1,850.
It's jungle now.
I got into a pine tree zone there for a little bit.
But I'm in a valley.
A steep, cliffy valley.
Just went through about a half dozen tunnels.
But they're short tunnels.
The road's in good shape, too.
So I bottomed out.
Now I'm going up a river.
As you can see, it's pink.
I wonder how much it cost them to put the pink dye in.
So anyways, that would be a cool place to river.
Actually, I saw signs for river rafting.
It would be cool to swim down this and follow the rapids.
I want to do that one day.
But you've got houses on the side of the river.
This is southern Bosnia.
I don't know how far from...it's been raining on and off all day.
I don't know how far from Montenegro or whatever that next town is called.
We've got a road on the other side of the river.
So there you go.
Check out the size of this road I'm on in southern Bosnia.
I hope it's the right road.
Tiny little road.
There's like almost no traffic to it.
It's like kind of drizzling a little bit.
Get some houses up on the side of the hill.
I mean, are there even roads to get there?
This is pretty intense.
